pages.text:
Overview
Databricks is a managed Apache Spark-based lakehouse platform unifying data
engineering, data science, and BI on top of open storage formats (Delta Lake). On Azure it
deploys into your subscription as Azure Databricks with native Entra ID and networking
integration.
Core Concepts
Concept
Details
Workspace
Control-plane UI for notebooks, jobs, repos — data plane runs in your
VNet
Cluster
Set of VMs running Spark; interactive (all-purpose) or job clusters
(ephemeral, cheaper)
Delta Lake
ACID transaction layer over Parquet — time travel, schema
enforcement, MERGE support
Unity Catalog
Centralized governance: catalog.schema.table namespace, fine-
grained access control, lineage
DBFS / Volumes
Distributed file storage abstraction; Volumes provide governed non-
tabular file access
Delta Live Tables
(DLT)
Declarative pipeline framework with built-in data quality
expectations
Medallion Architecture
Bronze (raw ingested data, schema-on-read) → Silver (cleaned, validated, conformed schema,
deduplicated) → Gold (business-level aggregates, ready for BI/ML consumption). Each layer is a Delta
table enabling incremental, auditable transformation.
C L O U D C H E AT S H E E T · D ATA P L AT F O R M
Databricks
Unified lakehouse platform for data engineering, ML, and analytics on Spark
Mazhar Khan — Cloud Security Portfolio
Page 1 of 3

Security & Governance
Unity Catalog — table/column/row-level ACLs, tag-based access, cross-workspace data
lineage
VNet injection (Azure) — deploy clusters into your own subnets for NSG/firewall control
Secret scopes — backed by Azure Key Vault; avoid hardcoding credentials in
notebooks
Cluster policies — enforce instance types, autotermination, and tag compliance for
cost/security control
Private Link — front-end (UI/API) and back-end (data plane to control plane) private
connectivity
Audit logs — diagnostic logs stream to Log Analytics/Event Hub for compliance
monitoring
PySpark Essentials
Operation
Code Pattern
Read Delta table
spark.read.format("delta").load(path)
Write/merge
DeltaTable.forPath(spark, path).merge(...)
Time travel
spark.read.option("versionAsOf", 3).load(path)
Optimize + Z-order
OPTIMIZE table ZORDER BY (col)
Vacuum
VACUUM table RETAIN 168 HOURS — reclaims stale files
Structured
Streaming
readStream.format("delta")...writeStream.trigger(availableNow=True)
CI/CD & MLOps Integration
Databricks Asset Bundles — YAML-based IaC for jobs/pipelines, deployed via CLI in CI/
CD
MLflow — native experiment tracking, model registry, and stage transitions (Staging →
Production)
Repos — Git-backed notebooks for version control and PR-based review workflows
Workflows/Jobs API — orchestrate multi-task DAGs, integrate with ADF or external
schedulers
•

Mazhar Khan — Cloud Security Portfolio
Page 2 of 3

Common Pitfalls
Pitfall
Fix
Small files problem
degrading query
performance
Run OPTIMIZE regularly; use auto-compaction and Z-ordering on filter
columns
All-purpose clusters
left running idle
Enforce autotermination via cluster policies; prefer job clusters for
scheduled work
Notebooks with
hardcoded
credentials
Use secret scopes backed by Key Vault; never inline connection
strings
No lineage/
governance across
workspaces
Adopt Unity Catalog early; retrofitting from Hive metastore later is
costly
Skipping VACUUM
causing storage
bloat
Schedule VACUUM with a sensible retention window (min 7 days for
time-travel safety)
Interview / Exam Quick Hits
Delta Lake provides ACID guarantees that raw Parquet on a data lake does not
Job clusters are cheaper than all-purpose clusters because they terminate
automatically after the job
Unity Catalog centralizes governance across workspaces, replacing legacy per-
workspace Hive metastore
Photon is Databricks' native vectorized query engine accelerating SQL and
DataFrame workloads
Auto-scaling clusters balance cost vs. performance but add startup latency — job
clusters mitigate with pools
